name: Phison PS3111-S11
notes: Budget DRAM-less SATA. Used in Kingston A400, Patriot Burst, PNY CS900, many OEM/white-label drives. Known SATAFIRM S11 firmware issue.

name: Phison PS3112-S12
notes: Mid-range SATA with DRAM. Better sustained writes than S11. Used in Patriot, Corsair, some Kingston drives.

name: Phison PS3112-S12DC
notes: Enterprise SATA QLC datacenter controller. Power-loss protection, end-to-end data protection. World's highest capacity 2.5" SATA (15.36TB). Shipping since Aug 2020. Read-intensive, 0.1 DWPD. Custom firmware for OEMs.

name: Phison PS3113-S13T
notes: Newer S11 successor. 4-channel DRAM-less. Validated for QLC. Used in entry-level SATA SSDs.

name: Phison PS5031-E31T
notes: First Gen5 mainstream DRAM-less controller. 34% lower power than Gen4 at same performance. Great low-QD random performance. Used in Crucial P510, Inland QN446, many budget Gen5 drives. Also runs at Gen4 speeds in Gen4 systems.

name: Phison E37T
notes: CES 2026 announcement. 14.7 GB/s at under 2.3W — addresses PCIe 5.0 power concerns. DRAM-less, single-sided. Ideal for laptops, handhelds, 2230/2242/2280. Supports 4800 MT/s NAND (38% gain over prior gen). Not yet in shipping drives.

name: Phison PS5028-E28
notes: Flagship Gen5 consumer/prosumer. 6nm TSMC, 8-channel, DRAM. Used by Corsair MP700 Pro XT, ADATA XPG Blade, TeamGroup Z54E, Sabrent Rocket 5. 8TB drives coming. Also basis for aiDAPTIV+ AI100E (SLC variant).

name: Phison X2 (Enterprise 16-ch)
notes: Enterprise 16-channel controller. Powers all Pascari SSDs: X201 (performance, up to 30.72TB TLC), D201 (density, 15.36TB E1.S), D205V (world's first 128TB QLC SSD). U.2, E3.S, E3.L form factors.

name: Samsung MKX
notes: Samsung in-house SATA controller. Used in 870 EVO. Samsung reportedly discontinuing SATA line.

name: Samsung Pascal
notes: Samsung in-house Gen5 controller. Used in 9100 Pro. Only available in Samsung drives (vertically integrated).

name: Silicon Motion SM2259
notes: Competitor to Phison S12. Used in industrial SSDs (Apacer, Innodisk). 4-channel DRAM-backed.
